footer.description: Established in 1998, dedicated to education aid and school building in Guizhou's impoverished mountainous areas. Zero admin fee, 100% donations directly reach the needy.

home.heroSubtitle: Since 1998, we have been dedicated to building schools and providing education aid in impoverished mountainous areas of Guizhou, China. Zero admin fees, 100% of donations go directly to those in need.
home.aboutTitle: Love and Care for Those in Need
home.aboutText: Sin Kai Charity Fund was established in 1998 by a group of patriotic volunteers. We are non-religious, politically neutral, and purely dedicated to social charity services. Registered as a non-profit charity in Hong Kong, we guarantee zero admin fees — every dollar donated goes 100% to poverty alleviation projects.
home.statSchoolsDesc: Hope schools built with mooncake proceeds
home.statDonationDesc: Zero admin fee, all to charity projects

about.pageSubtitle: Non-religious, politically neutral, purely dedicated to social charity services
about.backgroundTitle: Starting from a Group of Volunteers in 1995
about.backgroundText1: In early 1995, a small group of patriotic volunteers participated in poverty alleviation work in China's impoverished mountainous areas, witnessing the barren wastelands. In these cold, hungry and deprived mountains, abandoned orphans, left-behind children, single-parent families, elderly without support, the disabled, and those with critical illnesses left deep impressions on every volunteer.
about.backgroundText2: Moved by what they saw, the volunteers actively worked on poverty alleviation and education aid in these areas. After extensive deliberation on goals and mission, they decided in 1998 to establish a charitable organisation named 'Sin Kai Charity Fund'.
about.plan1Text: Sponsoring impoverished primary, secondary, university and postgraduate students through the 'Dream Programme'. Student data comes from volunteer records during school visits in Guizhou's impoverished areas, or recommendations from school principals and village committees. Orphans and disabled students are given priority.
about.plan2Text: Assisting local governments in building schools, canteens, water cisterns, sports facilities and more in impoverished mountainous areas. Each school requires matching funds from local government, with impoverished farmers contributing labour. Donations are paid in three instalments tied to construction milestones.
about.plan3Text: Helping disaster-affected residents, providing subsidies to elderly widows, assisting orphans, single-parent families and extreme hardship households. Also providing repair aid for dilapidated housing, medical assistance for critically ill villagers, building water cisterns for drought areas, and repairing flood-damaged riverbanks and bridges.
about.guizhouText1: Guizhou Province covers 67,992 square kilometres, about 1.8% of China's total area. Located inland, 92.5% of the province is highland mountains, with rugged stone mountains, steep valleys and underground rivers at altitudes of 1,000 to 2,900 metres.
about.guizhouText2: Due to barren mountain soil, most areas are unsuitable for farming. Only a small portion of terraced fields near water sources at mountain feet are cultivable, yielding just one harvest per year. Guizhou remains one of China's poorest regions, with severe shortages of basic supplies and healthcare.
about.achievementsTitle: 27 Years of Persistence, Countless Lives Changed
about.achievement1: Build schools in Guizhou's impoverished areas
about.achievement2: Sponsor impoverished students through Dream Programme
about.achievement3: Mid-Autumn elderly care events in Hong Kong
about.quoteText: Throughout the journey, I realised how much the poor communities behind our prosperous society need our care, and witnessed how selflessly Sin Kai's volunteers fight for education opportunities for impoverished students.
about.quoteAuthor: — Mr. Ho Hon Leung, Belgian Chinese

gallery.pageSubtitle: Since 1998, Sin Kai Charity Fund has conducted countless charity activities in Guizhou's impoverished mountainous areas and Hong Kong. Here are our precious records.
gallery.snowDisasterDesc: In 2008, severe snowstorms devastated Guizhou Province. Sin Kai quickly organised relief efforts, distributing over a thousand bags of rice and two thousand quilts to 2,300+ extreme hardship households.
gallery.oldSchoolsDesc: These are the real conditions of schools in Guizhou's mountainous areas before Sin Kai's aid — mud-built dangerous structures, broken roofs, dim classrooms, yet the only place where countless children could access knowledge.
gallery.newSchoolsDesc: Through Sin Kai and generous donors' efforts, new Hope Primary Schools have been built in Guizhou's mountainous areas. Children finally have safe, bright learning environments.
gallery.fieldTripDesc: Sin Kai volunteers visit Guizhou's impoverished mountainous areas multiple times each year, personally understanding villagers' needs, recording impoverished students' information, and monitoring school construction progress.
gallery.hkCharityDesc: Each year before the Mid-Autumn Festival, Sin Kai organises the 'Mid-Autumn Love for the Elderly' event in multiple Hong Kong housing estates, distributing festival mooncakes, fruit and daily necessities to elderly residents living alone.
gallery.mountainDesc: Guizhou Province is located on a plateau, 92.5% mountainous. These photos truthfully document the living conditions of impoverished villagers — rugged mountain roads, dilapidated houses, and rudimentary facilities.
gallery.activitiesDesc: Sin Kai regularly organises multiple annual activities, including Guizhou field trips, Mid-Autumn elderly care events, charity mooncake sales, and more, continuously bringing warmth and hope to impoverished mountainous areas.
gallery.quote1: Throughout the journey, I realised how much the poor communities behind our prosperous society need our care, and witnessed how selflessly Sin Kai's volunteers fight for education opportunities for impoverished students.
gallery.author1: — Mr. Ho Hon Leung, Belgian Chinese
gallery.quote2: Sin Kai carefully handles each Hope Primary School, conducting multiple field surveys before execution; whether climbing mountains, crossing rivers, or walking ten to eight hours on foot. These volunteers give not only their love but also their money and time.
gallery.newsDesc: Each year before the Mid-Autumn Festival, Sin Kai sells charity mooncakes with all proceeds going to school building in Guizhou's impoverished mountainous areas. Over the years, 18 Hope Schools have been built with mooncake proceeds.

blog.pageSubtitle: Learn more about Guizhou's impoverished mountainous areas, Sin Kai's work, and the stories behind our charity efforts.

donate.pageSubtitle: Every dollar you donate will go 100% to our charity projects
donate.methodsDesc: Generous donors may deposit donations directly into our charity account, or contact us by phone. Please retain the deposit slip, write the donor's name and contact number, and fax it to us. Tax receipts are issued for donations of HK$100 or more.
donate.inPersonAddress: Unit 03, 15/F, Charm Centre, 700 Castle Peak Road, Lai Chi Kok, Kowloon
donate.inPersonNote: Lai Chi Kok MTR Station Exit C
donate.schoolBuildingDesc: Assist local governments in building schools, canteens, water cisterns and sports facilities in impoverished mountainous areas
donate.studentAidDesc: Sponsor primary, secondary and university students through the Dream Programme
donate.emergencyDesc: Help disaster victims, provide subsidies to elderly widows, and assist extreme hardship households
donate.mooncakeDesc: Each year before the Mid-Autumn Festival, Sin Kai sells charity mooncakes with all proceeds going to school building in Guizhou. Over the years, 18 Hope Schools have been built with mooncake proceeds.
mooncakeSpecs: Type: Double Yolk White Lotus Seed Paste Mooncake
Packaging: Premium gift box, 4 pieces per box, net weight 750g
Production: 100% Hong Kong made by experienced chefs at 'Light Vegetarian Restaurant'
Price: HK$200 per box, free delivery for 20+ boxes
donate.accountNote: Please retain the deposit slip. Tax receipts issued for HK$100+ donations.
